neighborhoods.name: Centro Storico (Palermo)
neighborhoods.description: Chaotic, fascinating historic center with Arab-Norman UNESCO sites, street food markets, and baroque churches
neighborhoods.character: Arab-Norman UNESCO, market culture, street food capital

housing.predominantType: Apartments in baroque palazzi and restored buildings

neighborhoods.character_notes: The historic and commercial heart of Palermo. Most walkable area with best access to cultural attractions, restaurants, and public services.

neighborhoods.name: Mondello
neighborhoods.description: Beach neighborhood north of Palermo with Art Nouveau villas, sandy beach, and seafood restaurants
neighborhoods.character: Beach residential, Art Nouveau, seafood culture

housing.predominantType: Apartments, Art Nouveau villas, and beach houses

neighborhoods.character_notes: Established residential area offering a quieter lifestyle while maintaining reasonable access to Palermo's center and services.

neighborhoods.name: Cefalu / Castelbuono
neighborhoods.description: Coastal and mountain towns east of Palermo with lower costs and rich Norman heritage

housing.predominantType: Stone houses, apartments, and village properties

neighborhoods.character_notes: More affordable option for budget-conscious retirees. May require a car or longer transit times to reach central amenities.

neighborhoods.name: Taormina Area
neighborhoods.description: Famous hilltop resort town on the east coast with ancient Greek theater and international glamour
neighborhoods.character: Glamorous hilltop, ancient Greek theater, international

housing.predominantType: Elegant apartments, villas, and historic properties

neighborhoods.character_notes: Area with established international community and expat-oriented services. English is more commonly spoken here than in other neighborhoods.
